content: Mars has two small natural satellites, or moons. Their names are Phobos and Deimos. Both moons were discovered in 1877 and are named after the Greek mythological characters Phobos (Fear) and Deimos (Panic), who were the sons of Ares (the Greek equivalent of Mars).
